data.title: Diversified Energy Prices Public Share Offer to Raise $123 Million
data.description:
Diversified Energy priced its previously disclosed underwritten public offering of up to 8.5 million shares at $14.50 apiece, according to a Thursday filing.

The transaction aims to raise $123.3 million, which will be used to partly repay the debt that the London-listed energy company expects to incur under its planned acquisition of Maverick Natural Resources, or for general corporate purposes if the acquisition does not close.

Completion of the share offer is expected on Friday, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Singapore's Wilmar misses annual profit view on weak feed, plantation segments
data.description:
Singaporean food conglomerate Wilmar International reported a bigger-than-expected fall in full-year core net profit on Thursday, hurt by weak performance from its plantation and sugar milling as well as feed and industrial products segments.

While the contribution from the firm's oilseeds business improved towards the end of the second half, continued challenging operating conditions for its tropical oils business and lower production and yield of fresh fruit bunches in the same period weighed on Wilmar's profits, the company said.

The company, one of the world's largest food producers, reported a 23.3% decline in core net profit at $1.16 billion for the twelve months ended December 31. Analysts had expected $1.40 billion profit, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company also proposed a final dividend of S$0.10 per share, slightly lower than S$0.11 per share declared last year.

data.title: Mitsui-Soko Holdings' Nine Month Profit Falls 10%
data.description:
Mitsui-Soko Holdings' profit attributable to owners of the parent fell 10.2% to 8.96 billion yen in the nine months ended Dec 31, from 9.98 billion yen a year ago mainly due to higher expenses.

The marine cargo handling company's basic earnings per share fell to 359.48 yen from 400.65 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing.

Operating revenue, however, rose 7.4% to 212 billion yen from 197.5 billion yen a year earlier.

It forecasted attributable profit to decline 17.4% to 10 billion yen or 401.12 yen per basic share, and operating revenue to gain 7.4% to 280 billion yen for the fiscal year ending Mar 31.

The company expects to pay a year-end dividend of 73 yen per share for the current fiscal year​.

data.title: Haier Group to acquire near 42% stake in Autohome for $1.8 billion
data.description: Hong Kong-listed Autohome said on Thursday its controlling shareholder Yun Chen Capital Cayman has agreed to sell 41.91% stake in the company to a unit of Haier Group Corp for $1.8 billion.

data.title: UniCredit-Commerzbank Deal More Likely to Happen if Center Right Wins German Elections — Market Talk
data.description: A center-right government would be the most favorable toward a deal between Italy's UniCredit and German lender Commerzbank, Morningstar analyst Michael Field says in a market comment ahead of Sunday's elections in Germany. "Their voter base is generally less concerned about protecting workers from job losses at all costs as a result of a takeover," he notes. UniCredit has recently built up a 28% stake in Commerzbank and signaled its intention to pursue a tie-up, triggering political opposition in Berlin. The Alliance 90/The Greens have been the most open toward a deal but their inclusion in a coalition isn't guaranteed, Field says. The parties that are likely to enter the parliament have voiced concerns about the Italian lender's approach, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: India Glycols Bags Ethanol Supply Order
data.description:
India Glycols said it had been allocated 180.6 million liters of ethanol under the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme from Nov. 1, 2024, to Oct. 31, 2025.

The total value of the allocation is estimated at 12.6 billion Indian rupees, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The contract was awarded by a consortium comprising Bharat Petroleum, Indian Oil, Hindustan Petroleum, Reliance Industries, and Nayara Energy.

data.title: Playmates Expects Swing to Loss in 2024
data.description:
Playmates expects a pre-tax loss of around HK$259 million in 2024, compared with a pre-tax profit of HK$170 million a year prior, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The real estate investor attributed the anticipated loss mainly to a higher unrealized revaluation deficit from the revaluation of its investment properties, as well as the impact of a lower profit of PTL Group on its performance.

data.title: Greece sets new record with 21.7-bln-euro tourism revenue in 2024
data.description:
Key points:
  • Greece's previous 2023 record was 20.6 bln euros
  • Tourist revenues partly offset drop in total exports
  • Current account deficit widens to 15 bln euros in 2024

Greece on Thursday set a new record by collecting 21.7 billion euros ($22.64 billion) in revenues from tourism last year, partly offsetting a drop in total exports of other services and goods and higher imports.

Tourism revenues came in line with the government's forecast and broke a previous record of 20.6 billion euros in 2023, data from the country's central bank, the Bank of Greece, showed on Thursday.

Despite summer wildfires and hotter weather which have become more frequent at Europe's southernmost tip, arrivals of foreign visitors rose by an annual 9.8% last year, the central bank said. Some 36 million tourists had visited the Mediterranean country in 2023.

Tourism is a key economic driver for Greece as it accounts for more than a quarter of its economic output.

But higher tourism revenues last year only partly offset rising imports and declining exports of services and goods. As a result, the current account deficit stood at 15 billion euros in 2024, up from a deficit of 13.9 billion euros in 2023.

High seismic activity on the island of Santorini, popular for its white-washed houses and breathtaking sunsets into the Aegean Sea, in recent weeks has sparked some concerns over the upcoming tourist season.

Tens of thousands of mild quakes have been recorded off the volcanic island since late January, forcing thousands of people to flee and authorities to shut schools and ban construction activity.

No major damage has been reported on Santorini so far and activity has weakened from last week but seismologists have not ruled out a bigger quake.

Greece's tourist season traditionally kicks off in April before peaking in the summer. Greece will monitor the quakes for a few weeks before deciding on measures that will guarantee the safety of foreign visitors, Greece's tourism minister Olga Kefalogianni said on Thursday.

"It's important for us to show that we have the capacity to deal with whatever happens," she told public broadcaster ERT.

($1 = 0.9585 euros)

data.title: German debt brake: don't forget the escape clause
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 up 0.3%
  • Miners lead
  • Defence stocks edge lower
  • Wall St futures down

GERMAN DEBT BRAKE: DON'T FORGET THE ESCAPE CLAUSE

Whether a new German government will reform the debt brake and increase military spending is the hot topic ahead of German elections this weekend.

The key risk to a reform of the debt brake comes from whether smaller parties polling around the 5% threshold make it to the Parliament (FDP, BSW, and the left), Citi analysts argue, flagging that it's not their base case scenario.

FDP is against the debt brake reform and, together with AfD, could form a blocking minority, while negotiations might be tricky with the left, which doesn't want more defence spending.

A two-thirds majority is required to change the rule, which prevents governments from expanding the fiscal deficit, and a blocking alliance of the AfD and BSW could make financing increased military spending all but impossible.

UBS economists say that to create significant fiscal space, Germany can trigger the escape clause, which states that the debt brake can be deactivated in cases of "exceptional emergency situations" outside the government's control and have significantly adverse effects on public finances.

The escape clause, triggered by a simple majority in Parliament, removes deficit limits, while the EU fiscal rules remain in place.

However, the European Commission will propose exempting defence from EU limits on government spending, the head of the EU executive, Ursula von der Leyen, said on Friday, amid pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump for Europe to finance its own defence.

Citi economists’ base case is for a CDU/CSU coalition with either SPD or greens, with a €100bn infra/defence fund for 3 years and by then debt brake reform to allow 1% of structural deficit/GDP ratio from 2028.

(Stefano Rebaudo)

*****

data.title: Market Chatter: CJ Cheiljedang Expands Sales to US Army Bases Worldwide
data.description:
South Korean food company CJ Cheiljedang aims to sell its Bibigo dumplings, Hetbahn rice, and tteokbokki at all US Army bases globally amid rising demand for Korean food, Yonhap News Agency reported Wednesday.

The company recently launched its products at the US base in Guam, following their introduction at eight bases in South Korea in April 2024, according to the report.

Plans are underway to expand the supply of CJ Cheiljedang products to bases in Japan, Hawaii, Alaska, and beyond, the report said.

Shares of CJ Cheiljedang rose nearly 2% at market close on Thursday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Jiangsu High Hope International Group Approves One-Year Extension for Investment in Huijing Yongkang Fund
data.description:
Jiangsu High Hope International Group approved a one-year extension for subsidiary Huihong Venture Capital's investment in the Huijing Yongkang Fund.

The fund was established to support a public-private partnership hospital project in Nanjing, China.

The extension aligns with the delayed construction timeline of the fourth affiliated hospital of the Nanjing Medical University project.

data.title: Pantheon Resources Names New CEO
data.description:
Pantheon Resources appointed Max Easley as its new chief executive officer, according to a Thursday filing.

Easley will replace Jay Cheatham, who will temporarily remain in the oil and gas company as a nonexecutive director to ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities.

data.title: Malaysia's Export Growth Could Slow This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's export growth could slow this year as various risks prompt caution, UOB economists Julia Goh and Loke Siew Ting say in a note. They forecast 2025 export growth at 4.5%, down from 5.7% in 2024. The UOB economists note potential U.S. tariffs on semiconductors could significantly impact Malaysia, where electronics account for 41.1% of exports. The semiconductor cycle peaked in 3Q last year, signaling a downturn thereafter, while mining exports continue to decline, they say. Volatile commodity prices and forex fluctuations add to the uncertainty, UOB says. Softening imports of intermediate goods and declining PMI for new export orders suggest cautious sentiment among exporters, UOB adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Da Yu Financial Holdings Expects to Swing to Profit in 2024
data.description:
Da Yu Financial Holdings expects to swing to a net profit of HK$21 million for 2024 from a net loss of HK$162 million in 2023, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The company attributed the expected turnaround to increases in bond investment and fee incomes and the lack of a HK$172 million non-cash goodwill impairment during the reporting year.

data.title: Scania, DHL to Test Electric Truck With Fuel-powered Generator
data.description:
Truck manufacturer Traton's subsidiary Scania plans to test an electric truck with a fuel-powered generator developed in partnership with Deutsche Post , d/b/a DHL Group.

The generator will extend the truck's range by shifting to battery-electric road transport without having to wait for a complete charging network, according to a Thursday release.

Logistics giant DHL's Post & Parcel Germany division will deploy the truck in February to test its performance.

data.title: Sobi, Apellis Seek EMA Nod for Expanded Use of Aspaveli in Rare Kidney Diseases
data.description:
Swedish Orphan Biovitrum , or Sobi, said Thursday the European Medicines Agency validated its joint application with Apellis Pharmaceuticals to extend the indication for Aspaveli to treat rare and chronic kidney diseases.

The submission backed by data from the Phase 3 Valiant study shows a statistically significant 68% reduction in proteinuria in pegcetacoplan-treated patients, compared with placebo, the biopharmaceutical company said.

The study results at week 26 are consistent and show that the drug stabilizes kidney functioning and demonstrates a favorable safety profile, according to a release.

data.title: KKR Secures Majority Stake in Fuji Soft as It Wins Bidding War With Bain Capital — Update
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

KKR has secured a majority stake in Fuji Soft and plans to take the company private, just days after rival Bain Capital dropped out of a monthslong bidding war for the Japanese software company, valued at more than $4 billion.

KKR announced Thursday that it would become the largest shareholder with a 58% stake following a successful tender offer and plans to acquire the remaining shares through a squeeze-out process.

Foreign private-equity firms like KKR and Bain Capital have been riding a growing wave of Japanese mergers and acquisitions in recent years, as large companies spin off noncore businesses or managements choose to leave the public market.

Earlier this month, KKR raised its tender-offer price to 9,850 yen, equivalent to $65.03, from Y9,451 in an attempt to end the takeover battle, and also extended the tender-offer period through Wednesday.

In November, KKR acquired more than one-third of Fuji Soft's shares, securing shares held by two major shareholders, 3D Investment Partners and Farallon Capital Management.

"We are fully committed and look forward to supporting Fuji Soft's plan to enhance its corporate value, under a new and simpler ownership by KKR," said Hiro Hirano, deputy executive chairman of KKR Asia Pacific.

KKR estimates the minimum ownership stake required to conduct a squeeze-out will be around 53%, based on past voting patterns. A shareholders' meeting for the squeeze-out process is scheduled for late April.

Fuji Soft had nearly 18,000 employees at the end of 2023 and provides information-technology services to clients across a wide range of industries.

In 2024, the company's operating profit rose 6.5% to Y22 billion, equivalent to $145.2 million, as revenue grew 6.2% to Y317 billion, Fuji Soft said last week.

Bain Capital said Monday it decided not to begin a tender offer for the software company after consulting with Fuji Soft founder Hiroshi Nozawa, who intended to take the company private with Bain Capital. The U.S. private-equity firm said in December that it planned to start a tender offer at Y9,600 a share once certain conditions were met.

"Bain Capital wish for the continued growth of [Fuji Soft ] under the renewed governance led by the new shareholders," it said Monday.

Shares closed flat at Y9,795 on Thursday.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Accor Steps Up Buyback, Beating Expectations — Market Talk
data.description: Accor's plan to buy back 440 million euros in shares this year is ahead of both expectations and previous guidance, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The French hotel group in 2023 said it would return 3 billion euros to shareholders over the period to 2027 and the company had signaled this would include annual buyback programs of 400 million euros. A 440 million-euro buyback in 2025 adds 0.3% to Accor's earnings per share, according to Jefferies. Accor reaffirmed its midterm targets, but didn't provide guidance for 2025. The company's 2024 results leave it on track for its medium-term targets, with revenue ahead of expectations and Ebitda in line, Jefferies says. Shares fall 0.8%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Zurich Insurance CEO Keeps Buybacks Sparse — Market Talk
data.description: Share buybacks cannot be done every year, the CEO of Zurich Insurance says in a results call with reporters. The Swiss insurer paid out a 28 Swiss franc dividend per share for 2024, corresponding to its 75% profit payout policy. "There is no space with such a dividend policy to have a yearly buyback," Mario Greco said. The company will conduct buybacks in the case of extraordinary transactions, as seen two years ago, or if it accumulates cash and excess capital that it needs to return. This echoes the strategy Zurich outlined in November when it laid out 2025-2027 targets. In the same call, CFO Claudia Cordioli flagged "extremely attractive opportunities" to invest capital back in the business to support growth. Shares rise 3% to highs last seen in 2001. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Shares Gaining
data.description:
Stocks on the rise in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0944 GMT - Centrica posted an overall positive update, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. The U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, said its was extending its share buyback and provided clearer information on capital allocation. Earnings per share and net cash came in above consensus estimates and the dividend payout was in line, the analysts say. Centrica also gave a detailed plan on how to reduce net debt, the analysts say. Shares trade 8% higher at 146.70 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0941 GMT - Rio Tinto's 2024 results were broadly in line with expectations and the Anglo-Australian mining company provided a steady outlook for the future, energy and materials analyst at Quilter Cheviot Maurizio Carulli writes. It is reassuring to see that group earnings decreased by only 2% despite an 8% fall in iron-ore prices, he writes. While acquisitions pushed net debt up to $5.5 billion in 2024, it remains at comfortable level, he says. The miner boasts a solid portfolio of assets and the significant barriers to entry in iron ore and copper strengthen Rio Tinto's market position, he writes. Rio Tinto's London listed shares rise 0.75% to 5,074.00 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0859 GMT - Anglo American's $2.9 billion write down of its De Beers unit was in-line with expectations, RBC Capital Markets Ben Davis and Marina Calero write. Anglo's simplification efforts mean it is no longer a bid target given its share-price rise, the analysts write. The shares trade up 38% over the past year and only trade at an around 2.5% discount to the final offer BHP made last year, they add. The miner also made a successful step forward by agreeing a memorandum of understanding with Codelco to implement a joint mine plan between Los Bronces and Andina, which will deliver straightforward synergies, they write. Shares trade up 3.65% at 2,456.50 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0808 GMT - Lloyds Banking Group's update included comforting results though there were a lot of parts to digest, Jefferies says in a research note. The British bank took a provision linked to its motor finance business and issued guidance for 2025. Its 2026 guidance, which is more than 15% higher than consensus, was maintained. "The market may start to take more comfort in this today, given guidance that hedge income is expected to increase circa 2.7 billion pounds between 2024 and 2026," analyst Jonathan Pierce and associate Priya Rathod write. The motor finance risk will hang over the shares for a little longer, they add. Shares open 2% higher at around 64 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Brokers Comments
data.description:
Broker comments in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0944 GMT - Centrica posted an overall positive update, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. The U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, said its was extending its share buyback and provided clearer information on capital allocation. Earnings per share and net cash came in above consensus estimates and the dividend payout was in line, the analysts say. Centrica also gave a detailed plan on how to reduce net debt, the analysts say. Shares trade 8% higher at 146.70 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0943 GMT - Hays ended the first half on a weak note, particularly in Germany and France, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. This is likely going to weigh on profit in the second half, the analysts say. The U.K. recruiter could expect forecasts for adjusted Ebita revised downward, they say. Consensus currently expects Hays to post full-year adjusted Ebita of around 66 million pounds. While increasing uncertainty makes it difficult to predict the company's financial performance in the short term, its strategic actions could help drive a medium-term improvement in earnings, Jefferies says. Shares are down 0.7% at 72.55 pence. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

The miner also made a successful step forward by agreeing a memorandum of understanding with Codelco to implement a joint mine plan between Los Bronces and Andina, which will deliver straightforward synergies, they write. Shares trade up 3.65% at 2,456.50 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0808 GMT - Lloyds Banking Group's update included comforting results though there were a lot of parts to digest, Jefferies says in a research note. The motor finance risk will hang over the shares for a little longer, they add. Shares open 2% higher at around 64 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0653 GMT - London Stock Exchange Group's shares are set up well to perform this year, RBC Capital Markets analysts Ben Bathurst and Jude Neanor write in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company is expected to deliver sustained strong underlying earnings and free cash-flow growth, supported by recent investments and acquisitions, the analysts say. "Given strong expected cash generation and leverage at the middle of the target range, we also can envisage a further capital return of around 1 billion pounds in buybacks," the Canadian bank adds. Shares closed at 117.05 pounds on Wednesday. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Centrica's Update Looks Positive — Market Talk
data.description: Centrica posted an overall positive update, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. The U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, said its was extending its share buyback and provided clearer information on capital allocation. Earnings per share and net cash came in above consensus estimates and the dividend payout was in line, the analysts say. Centrica also gave a detailed plan on how to reduce net debt, the analysts say. Shares trade 8% higher at 146.70 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)
